title: Goat Cheese Polish Casserole
description: A rich and creamy casserole featuring the tangy flavors of goat cheese, perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: In a large skillet, cook the Polish sausage over medium-high heat until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the crumbly goat cheese to the saucepan and stir until melted and smooth.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cubed Polish bread, cooked sausage mixture, and melted goat cheese mixture.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and dot the top with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the casserole.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the casserole is golden brown and the cheese is bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
